entries.name: The Crossing — ~8 million New Yorkers torn into Hethrun in one black second; what did it, and is there a way home?
Find your people — brother Akhil and the ~50, scattered across a realm at war; finding them needs a city, news, and leverage

KNOWN drift-marked — the secret is OUT. Shake owned it to his men, but it COST him (Fate No): Hethrun's 'drift-tainted' prejudice bit — muttering, a couple of desertions, his moral authority dented at the worst hour; only Eadwig's vouching held it; labels ('drift-blessed') & a knighting didn't (Fate No) — only a DEED would. THE DEED CAME: breaking a 3:1 siege, saving the heir & taking the warlord, before a thousand witnesses — the drift-tainted muttering is buried under the legend of the drift-marked knight who won the impossible. Standing among his own = REDEEMED. (The hostile Crown still knows what he is — a different, higher problem.)

Sworn man of the line — RESOLVED toward loyalty: at the moment he could have kept Marlford for himself, Shake knelt & re-pledged his AND Marlford's fealty to Aldwin (held of the line, not seized). His ambition is now LAUNDERED into legitimacy — the loyal kingmaker/right-hand, not a usurper warlord; Aldwin named him to 'stand beside me, always.' (Power held through loyalty — far stronger vs the Crown's 'upstart' framing.)

The line HELD — the decapitation campaign FAILED at its climax: Shake reached Aldwin a step ahead of the knife & saved him; Eadgyth lives & is allied. Edmund (stricken) + Aldwin + Eadgyth survive; the blood endures. (Edmund still needs a settled regency/future; the line's survival is now a political fact to defend at the Witan.)

THE RELIEF RIDE — WON. Shake marched alone, caught the besiegers massed & surprised (total surprise), and broke the siege of Hollowford with a hammer of 40 lances; saved Aldwin personally (killed the Bone-Sayer on the tower stair); Soren's inside-coup collapsed (Wulf's men killed him); the **Ash-Captain CAPTURED ALIVE** (a living witness/proof). The relief's purpose achieved — the line endures, the siege lifted, the enemy host scattered. (Aftermath: secure the keep & March; the war now moves to the political endgame.)

The Coronation Reckoning — the Witan settles the realm at MIDWINTER (now ~imminent). The relief's victory + a CAPTURED Ostmark warlord + web-captives give Shake real cards — PROOF of Ranulf's proxy war to wave at the table — but the Crown already brands him a drift-marked upstart warlord. The political endgame: turn a battlefield win into a standing in the realm (or a target on his back)

The wary March — battlefield-hero ≠ political power. Despite the relief & the recovered plunder, the March's thanes HEDGE (Fate No): drift-marked unease among the gentry + the hostile Crown + no Beorhtric to lead them in + the county's bred self-interest. Shake holds the CENTER (Hollowford/Aldwin/Marlford + his own sworn men & host) but NOT the March's landed strength. Winning the gentry — by deeds, the Witan, or courting Harbury's moot — is the unfinished base of his power

The web / Ranulf's hand — its Hollowford endgame FOILED (assassin dead, Soren's coup broken, Ash-Captain captured). But the spider RAN HOME: Cray, the Marlford head & the grain-factor slipped the pursuit WEST toward Mossdrowen (uncaught) to report to the paymaster (cui-bono: EORL RANULF, untouched). Shake holds PROOF & captives to take UP the chain — to the Witan, or west on the cold-but-live Mossdrowen trail. (GM-truth: Ranulf→Greldon→Bone-Sayers)
